concerns.analysis: The source PDF consists of 14 distinct pages/slides. The output JSON explicitly contains a placeholder stating '7 sections omitted for token budget'. Consequently, the content from pages 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10 is entirely absent from the output. This includes the goals slide, operations overviews, ground truth locations, planning maps, and survey activity slides.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains 14 pages, including slides titled 'National Coastal Mapping Program Goals' (p4), '2016 Post-Matthew Operations Overview' (p5), 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' (p6), 'Post-Matthew Data Production Status Map' (p7), '2017 NCMP Planning' (p9), and 'NOAA Channel survey activities' (p10).

concerns.analysis: The source document contains at least three tables: a large 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' table on page 6 (~23 rows), a 'Flight Block Status' table on page 7, and a data table on page 8. The output JSON contains zero tables, resulting in a 100% loss of tabular data rows from the source.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table on Page 6 with columns Longitude, Latitude, Location, State and approximately 23 rows of data. Table on Page 7 (Flight Block Status) and Page 8 (Elevation Difference).
concerns.location: Metadata and throughout sections (specifically missing from omitted pages 6, 7, 8)

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is generally consistent with the document's structure as a presentation. However, in the final section, the title of Page 14 ('Hurricane Matthew Request - Landsat') is included as a heading element with 'level': 2 inside the element list of Section 12. Since the section title itself acts as a major header (H2 level), nesting a sibling-level heading (level 2) within its elements creates an incorrect hierarchy. Page 14 represents a distinct topic and should have been rendered
concerns.output_quote: {"title": "Hatteras Inlet Satellite-Derived Bathy", "elements": [ ... { "type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Hurricane Matthew Request - Landsat" }, ... ]}

concerns.analysis: Virtually all meaningful graphical content from the source PDF is missing or failed to render in the HTML output, replaced by solid green or patterned placeholder boxes. This includes critical maps showing hurricane operations (Page 5), ground truth locations and data tables (Page 6), data production status (Page 7), complex data visualizations/charts (Page 8), planning maps (Page 9), and satellite imagery/bathymetry charts (Pages 11-14).
